content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Flood-fill Algorithm". Refer to LeetCode description: https://leetcode.com/problems/flood-fill/
content.inputFormat: image: a 2D array of integers, sr: the row of the starting pixel, sc: the column of the starting pixel, newColor: the new color to fill
content.outputFormat: the modified image after applying the flood fill algorithm

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use a depth-first search or breadth-first search algorithm to traverse the image and fill the connected pixels with the new color
solution.timeComplexity: O(N*M) where N is the number of rows and M is the number of columns in the image
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N*M) in the worst case when the image is filled with the same color

code.Python3:
def floodFill(self, image: List[List[int]], sr: int, sc: int, newColor: int) -> List[List[int]]:
    oldColor = image[sr][sc]
    if oldColor == newColor: return image
    self.dfs(image, sr, sc, oldColor, newColor)
    return image

def dfs(self, image, sr, sc, oldColor, newColor):
    if sr < 0 or sr >= len(image) or sc < 0 or sc >= len(image[0]): return
    if image[sr][sc] != oldColor: return
    image[sr][sc] = newColor
    self.dfs(image, sr - 1, sc, oldColor, newColor)
    self.dfs(image, sr + 1, sc, oldColor, newColor)
    self.dfs(image, sr, sc - 1, oldColor, newColor)
    self.dfs(image, sr, sc + 1, oldColor, newColor)
